channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-48282 — Adobe ColdFusion Path Traversal Vulnerability
item.description: Adobe ColdFusion contains a path traversal vulnerability that could lead to arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user.

item.title: [Faible] Nayax visée par un « Syndicate » du cybercrime
item.description: Nayax visée par une revendication pirate massive : données de paiement, clients et risque cyber sous surveillance. 1 milliard de CB concernées ?

item.title: [Élevé] Chinese hackers develop LONGLEASH malware to expand ORB network
item.description: Chinese hackers tracked as 'UAT-7810' are actively evolving their malware to expand their Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by compromising internet-facing networking devices, primarily unpatched Ruckus routers. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Accenture confirms breach after hacker offers stolen data for sale
item.description: IT services giant Accenture has confirmed it suffered a security breach after a threat actor claimed to have stolen 35 GB of source code and other data from the company. [...]

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-56290 — Joomlack Page Builder Improper Access Control Vulnerability
item.description: Joomlack Page Builder contains an improper access control vulnerability that could allow for remote code execution via unauthenticated arbitrary file upload.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-55255 — Langflow Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key Vulnerability
item.description: Langflow contains an authorization bypass through user-controlled key vulnerability which allows an authenticated attacker to execute any flow belonging to another user by specifying the victim's flow ID in the request.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-48908 — JoomShaper SP Page Builder Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type Vulnerability
item.description: JoomShaper SP Page Builder contains an unrestricted upload of file with dangerous type vulnerability that allows unauthenticated users to upload arbitrary files, ultimately resulting in the upload and execution of PHP code.

item.title: [Moyen] New Januscape Linux flaw allows VM escape on Intel, AMD devices
item.description: A 16-year-old Linux kernel vulnerability, dubbed Januscape, allows attackers to escape a virtual machine and execute arbitrary code on the host. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Webinar tomorrow: Why modern email attacks require a new approach to defense
item.description: Tomorrow's webinar explores how behavioral AI can help organizations detect sophisticated phishing, business email compromise, and account takeover attacks while reducing alert fatigue through automated investigation…

item.title: [Faible] The GitHub Actions Attack Pattern Your CI Security Scanners Miss
item.description: ActiveState explains how GitHub Actions attack chains can evade traditional CI security scanners, why passing a scan doesn't guarantee a secure pipeline, and how organizations can better govern their CI/CD workflows.…

item.title: [Élevé] Spain arrests suspected member of pro-Russian hacktivist groups
item.description: The National Police in Spain have arrested a man who is suspected of being an active member of the CyberArmy of Russia Reborn (CARR) and Z-Pentest, both pro-Russian hacktivist groups. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Hidden backdoor in Tenda router firmware grants admin access
item.description: A hidden authentication backdoor has been found in multiple Tenda router firmware versions, potentially allowing an attacker to gain administrative access to the device's web management panel. [...]

item.title: [Critique] What Changes When Your Software Supply Chain Includes AI Writing Your Code?
item.description:
Software supply chain security was hard enough. Then AI joined the build pipeline.
For five years, "software supply chain security" meant one question: what's in your code? Which open-source packages, which versions,…

item.title: [Élevé] Writer AI Flaw Could Let Agent Previews Leak Session Tokens Across Tenants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched critical session isolation vulnerability in Writer, an enterprise generative artificial intelligence (AI) platform, that could result in cross-tenant…

item.title: [Faible] Court Filing Reveals Windows Device ID Helped FBI Trace Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker
item.description:
U.S. prosecutors linked an alleged Scattered Spider hacker to a break-in at a luxury jewelry retailer using a persistent Windows device ID, according to a newly unsealed federal complaint.
Microsoft records tied that…

item.title: [Faible] Public GitHub Issue Could Trick GitHub Agentic Workflows Into Leaking Private Repo Data
item.description:
A public issue can trick GitHub Agentic Workflows into leaking the contents of an organization's private repositories, researchers at Noma Security have shown.
The attacker needs only to open a normal-looking issue on…

item.title: [Faible] DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
item.description: A Microsoft 365 device code phishing campaign has been observed leveraging collaboration-themed lures to take control of victim accounts between the last week of June 2026 and into early July, per findings from…

item.title: [Élevé] Rogue Agent Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Hijack Google Dialogflow CX Chatbots
item.description:
A critical flaw in Google's Dialogflow CX could have let an attacker with edit rights on one Code Block-enabled agent compromise other Code Block-enabled agents in the same Google Cloud project.
From there, they could…

item.title: [Faible] RedWing MaaS Packages Android Bank Fraud as a Telegram Rental Service
item.description: A new Android malware operation called RedWing is being rented out on Telegram as a ready-made bank-fraud service. It lets even low-skill criminals take over a victim's phone, steal their banking logins, and capture the…

item.title: [Faible] Un ado de 15 ans accusé d’attaque cyber contre Bandai Channel
item.description: Un lycéen arrêté après une attaque ayant annulé 46 812 abonnements Bandai Channel. Il a utilisé ChatGPT pour créer son outil pirate.

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft testing new Cloud Rebuild Windows 11 recovery feature
item.description: Microsoft has begun testing the Cloud Rebuild recovery feature in the latest Windows 11 Insider Preview builds released for users in the Experimental channel. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] BeyondTrust warns of critical flaws in remote access software
item.description: BeyondTrust warned customers to patch two critical security flaws in its Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) software that could allow attackers to bypass authentication. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft to enable Windows settings backup by default for orgs
item.description: Microsoft says the Windows settings backup and restore tool will be enabled by default on Microsoft Entra-joined or Microsoft Entra hybrid-joined enterprise systems after upgrading to Windows 11 26H2. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] BeyondTrust Patches Critical Auth Bypass Flaws in Remote Support and PRA
item.description: BeyondTrust has released updates to address two critical security flaws affecting Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products that, if successfully exploited, could allow unauthenticated attackers to…

item.title: [Moyen] CERT/CC Warns of Hidden Admin Backdoor in Tenda Router Firmware
item.description: Several versions of firmware released by Chinese network device manufacturer Tenda have been found to embed an undocumented authentication backdoor that enables administrative access to the devices' web management…

item.title: [Critique] Suspected China-Aligned Hackers Exploit Roundcube Flaws Against Universities
item.description: A suspected China-aligned threat activity cluster has been observed exploiting Roundcube webmail software belonging to physics and engineering departments of U.S. and Canadian universities as part of a new…
